agent.runner_summary: The simulator adapter builds the SkyServe airline support agent and runs each turn with Runner.run. FixedTurn.content is the plain user message string passed to run_turn.

components.when_to_use: Booking lookup behavior, e.g. verifying a confirmation code returns the right passenger, route, and seat.
components.input_guidance: Pass the named argument confirmation_code (a demo code such as SKY123 or BAY777).
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the booking summary string returned to the agent, or 'No booking was found' for unknown codes.

components.when_to_use: Baggage policy answers for a given ticket type.
components.input_guidance: Pass the named argument ticket_type (e.g. basic, standard, business); it defaults to standard.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned policy string for the requested ticket type.

components.when_to_use: Seat-change behavior, including confirmation-code verification before mutating a booking.
components.input_guidance: Pass the named arguments confirmation_code and requested_seat.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the confirmation message; mock this tool so scenarios do not mutate shared booking state.

log_notes: Terminal sessions are saved as JSONL under logs/ with 'message' events carrying role and content; user 'content' values map directly to adapter-facing fixed turns.
learning_env_guidance: The agent-facing turn value is the plain user message string; put it directly in FixedTurn(content=...), not a JSON-encoded payload.

Prefer the OpenAI provider by setting OPENAI_API_KEY. If only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set, the agent uses the litellm/anthropic model path. For LLM-judge, persona, or generated-input models, prefer an OpenAI model via ModelSpec.
Mock airline_support.agent:change_seat in seat-change scenarios to keep runs deterministic and avoid mutating shared in-memory booking state.
